reason: The remediation only edits a PAM password quality configuration file to set the minclass parameter. This is a standard STIG hardening step, does not require a reboot, and poses minimal risk of system breakage. The only impact is stricter password composition, which may cause password changes to be rejected if they do not meet the new criteria, but it will not lock out existing accounts.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential for password change failures if minclass is set too high; User confusion if new complexity requirements are not communicated
